info.title: 4casters REST API
info.description:
Public REST API for the 4casters peer-to-peer betting exchange. Use this API to manage your account, query the orderbook and games, and place / edit / cancel orders.

All responses (unless noted otherwise) are JSON envelopes of the form `{ "data": ... }`.

tags.name: User
tags.description: Read account info, bets, and orders

tags.name: Orders
tags.description: Place, edit, look up, and cancel orders

tags.name: Markets
tags.description: Browse leagues, games, participants, and orderbooks

post.description: Initialize a session and obtain an auth token. Auth tokens are valid for 30 days, after which a fresh login is required. The token returned here can also be used to authenticate against the 4casters Streaming WebSocket API.

200.description: Login successful. The auth token is returned both as `data.user.auth` and as a signed `auth` cookie.

get.description: Return the currently-authenticated user's account information including balance, credit limit, current liability, default odds format, and access flags.
Return all of the caller's bets that have been matched but not yet graded.
Return all of the caller's open (unmatched) orders that are currently resting on the exchange and available to be matched.

For a given 4casters game ID, return the caller's open (unmatched) orders and matched (ungraded) bets for that game. Graded bets are not returned by this endpoint — use `/user/getGradedWagers` for those.

200.description: Orders and matched bets for the requested game

get.description: Return all of the caller's graded (settled) wagers between two dates, plus a summary of P&L and volume. Dates correspond to the **start time of the game** the wager is on (so `startDate=06-22-2022` returns wagers on games starting after 06-21-2022 00:00 GMT).

get.summary: Get bet by ID or txID
get.description: Look up a single bet (matched, graded, or open offer) belonging to the caller. Pass **either** `id` (the bet's `id`, e.g. from `/user/getMatchedBets`) **or** `txID` (the transaction id, e.g. from a `/session/v3/place` response).

parameters.name: txID
parameters.description: Transaction id (e.g. `wagerRequestID` or `txID` returned from place).

200.description: The bet, in matched, graded, or open form depending on its current state.

get.summary: Get bet by wager request ID
get.description:
Look up every bet tied to a single `wagerRequestID`. A `wagerRequestID` is server-generated for **each** order in a place request — use it to tie together every fill and resting offer that resulted from a single submitted order.

Returns three arrays:

- `unmatched` — open offers from the request that are still resting on the book (always empty for this endpoint; use `/user/getBetByUserReference` for current open offers).
- `matched`   — matched (ungraded) bets from the request.
- `graded`    — graded (settled) bets from the request, including risk, win, fees, and result.

Look up every bet tied to a `userReference`. `userReference` is an optional client-defined string you can attach to an order at place time to track it on your side.

Returns three arrays scoped to a single `gameID`:

- `unmatched` — open offers from this reference that are still resting on the book.
- `matched`   — matched (ungraded) bets from this reference.
- `graded`    — graded (settled) bets from this reference, including risk, win, fees, and result.

parameters.name: userReference
parameters.description: The client-defined reference passed in at place time.

408.description: Invalid User Reference or Game ID

post.description:
A safety mechanism: send a heartbeat with a `timeout` (in seconds). If the next heartbeat is not received within `timeout` seconds, **all of your open orders are cancelled**. Use this to ensure that orders are pulled if your client loses connectivity.

`timeout` must be greater than 5 seconds.

200.description: Heartbeat acknowledged. The response shape is forwarded from the orders service.

get.description: Calculate the caller's worst-case financial liability for a single game, taking into account every bet on every market in the game. The value is always `<= 0` — even a fully hedged book can incur loss from rainouts, suspensions, or disqualifications.

post.description:
Submit one or more orders. Per-order results are returned in `data.createdSessions`, **positionally** with the input `orders` array. Each entry is either a successful `{ matched, unmatched }` result or an `{ error, errorType }` failure.

See the dedicated [Place order](/pages/rest/orders/place-order) page for examples and the full set of order-type rules (`limit`, `post`, `postArb`, `fillAndKill`).

Change the price (`orderOdds`) and/or volume (`orderVolume`) of an existing **limit** order. For partially filled orders, 4casters cancels the existing order and submits a new one with the supplied values; the new `orderVolume` does **not** account for previously filled volume.

The newer gRPC variant `POST /session/v4/editOrder` accepts the same body shape and also returns `errorType` on validation failures.

200.description: Edit result. Mirrors the place-order response shape: a `cancelled` summary plus the new `matched` / `unmatched` outcome.

get.description: Get the current state of one of the caller's orders. Returns the order with `matchedRisk` / `unmatchedRisk` accounting and a denormalized snapshot of the underlying game.

post.description: Cancel a single open order by id. Returns the unfilled and filled amounts at cancellation time.

409.description: Order could not be cancelled (e.g. already fully filled)

post.description: Cancel a batch of orders by id. The `data` array is positional with the input `sessionIDs` and may contain a mix of successful cancels and failures.
Cancel every open order in the given game whose `userReference` matches one of the supplied references.

post.summary: Cancel all orders for a game
post.description: Cancel every open order on a single game. Optionally restrict to a single market `type`.

gameID.description: The game whose orders to cancel.

type.description: Optional. Cancel only orders of this market type.

post.summary: Cancel all orders for a league
post.description: Schedule cancellation of every open order in a league. This endpoint **dispatches asynchronously** — it acknowledges with `{ ordersReceived: true }` immediately and the cancels happen in the background.
Cancel **every** open order belonging to the caller across every game and league.

get.description:
Return the orderbook for a league or single game in **flat** form: each market is an array of orders rather than an object keyed by spread / total. This is the recommended orderbook endpoint.

Provide either `league` (entire league) or `gameID` (one game).

parameters.name: sport
parameters.description: Sport name, e.g. `basketball` (case-insensitive). Optional filter when `league` is set.

parameters.name: gameID
parameters.description: Single game id. When provided, `league` and `sport` are ignored.

200.description: Orderbook for one or more games

get.description: Return all active games for a league. This response does **not** include the orderbook or any prices — call `GET /exchange/v2/getOrderbook` for that.

parameters.name: league
parameters.description: League code from `GET /exchange/getLeagues`. Use `upcoming` for all upcoming games across leagues.

get.description: Return every league with active markets on the exchange. If the response includes a league containing `LIVE` (other than the per-sport live leagues `NFL-LIVE`, `NBA-LIVE`, `NCAAF-LIVE`, `NCAAB-LIVE`), the value `LIVE` is appended as an alias for fetching all in-play games.

parameters.name: sport
parameters.description: Optional sport filter (e.g. `basketball`, `soccer`, `golf`). When `sport=soccer` or `sport=golf`, `all` is appended to the list.

get.description: Return a deduplicated list of participants (teams, players, futures sides). When `active=true`, only participants of games starting in the future are returned.

parameters.name: active
parameters.description: When `true`, only return participants of games starting in the future.

post.description:
**Deprecated.** Use `GET /exchange/v2/getOrderbook` instead.

Returns the orderbook for an entire league in **nested** form, keyed by spread / total number. Kept for backwards compatibility with older clients.

get.description: Return the nested orderbook for a single game, plus any child games (props, alternate periods) and an `orderSummary` of the caller's own resting orders for the league.
Return commission paid to the caller by the players they have invited, grouped by player username. With no parameters, returns lifetime earnings; with `fromDate` / `toDate`, restricts the range.

commissionPaid.description: Each entry is a single-key object mapping a player's username to total commission paid.

bearerAuth.description: Pass your auth token in the `Authorization` header. The `Bearer` prefix is optional; the server also accepts a signed `auth` cookie or a `token` field in the request body.

GameID.name: gameID
GameID.description: A 4casters game id (`ObjectID`). Get game ids from `GET /exchange/v2/getGames` or `GET /exchange/v2/getOrderbook`.

CombinedWagers.description: Three-bucket wager view: open (`unmatched`), filled-but-not-graded (`matched`), and settled (`graded`).

MarketType.description: Market type. `moneyline1x2` is **soccer-only** (three-way money line: home / away / draw).

OrderType.description:
Order placement strategy.

- `limit` — default. Match against any liquidity at your price or better as a taker, rest the remainder as a maker.
- `post`  — guarantee maker. Reject if the order would match resting liquidity.
- `postArb` — like `post`, but allowed to match if the matched odds are within 1% of yours, avoiding taker fees.
- `fillAndKill` — guarantee taker. Match immediately or reject.

MarketSide.description:
Order side. Meaning depends on `type`:

- `moneyline`, `spread` — the participant id you are backing.
- `total` — `"over"` or `"under"`.
- `moneyline1x2` — `"yes"` or `"no"` on the outcome named by `market`.

ErrorType.description:
Programmatic per-order error class:

- `validation_error` — payload or state failed validation (missing fields, bad odds/number, invalid side, inactive game).
- `rejected_liability` — user/account/liability constraints prevented posting or execution.
- `rejected_order_type_rules` — order-type rules forbade execution (e.g. `fillAndKill` had no executable liquidity, `post` would match, or `postArb` is more than 1% away from the resting price).
- `system_error` — transient/internal error; retry may succeed.

error.description: Human-readable, non-stable description of the failure.

error_type.description: Legacy alias of `errorType` (still emitted by the WebSocket placeV3 path).

auth.description: Auth token. Pass as `Authorization: Bearer <auth>` on subsequent requests.

liability.description: Current open liability across all games.

commissionCharged.description: Commission rate charged to this account.

matchedVolume.description: Account preferences for displaying matched volume.

openInterest.description: Account preferences for displaying open interest.

defaultOffer.description: Default offer size when placing orders.

market.description: **`moneyline1x2` only.** Either `"draw"` or a participant id — names the outcome you're betting yes/no on.

bet.description: Amount to risk on the bet.

number.description: Spread or total number. Required for `spread` and `total`. Not used for `moneyline` or `moneyline1x2`.

expirationMinutes.description: Auto-cancel after N minutes. Omit to keep until game start.

userReference.description: Client-defined identifier preserved on every fill / offer derived from this order.

PlaceOrderResult.description: Either a successful place result or a per-order error. Returned positionally with the request `orders` array.

MatchedFill.description: A portion of an order that matched against existing liquidity.

number.description: Spread or total of the fill (`null` for moneylines).

orderID.description: Order id of the matched offer on the other side.

wagerRequestID.description: Server-generated id grouping every fill / offer derived from the input order.

win.description: Win amount, net of taker commission.

UnmatchedOffer.description: Resting offer created from the unfilled remainder of a place request. May be empty (`{}`) when the order matched fully.

orderID.description: Id of the new resting order.

orderOdds.description: New American odds. Pass `null` to leave odds unchanged.

orderVolume.description: New `bet` amount. Pass `null` to leave volume unchanged. **Does not** account for previously-filled volume.

intendedOrderVolume.description: Optional. The volume you originally intended to place; used by the server when reconciling partial fills.

blockIfNoOtherSideOrders.description: v4 only. Reject the edit if there is no resting opposite-side liquidity.

cancelled.description: Summary of the cancelled-and-replaced original order.

isTheBestOffer.description: True if there is no competing offer on the other side at better odds than this order.

odds.description: American odds of this order (sign-flipped from internal storage).

OU.description: Opposite of the order's side for `total` markets. `null` for non-total.

participant.description: Participant id you bet on. `null` for `total`.

matchedRisk.description: Risk that has already been matched (string-formatted, 2 decimals).

unmatchedRisk.description: Remaining risk still resting on the book.

reason.description: Failure reason. Present only when `success` is `false`.

odds.description: American odds of the cancelled order.

cheapDataUID.description: Stable cross-system identifier for the game (used for line correlation).

isSpecials.description: True for futures and other specials markets.

score.description: Final score; only present for ended games.

OrderbookOrder.description: A single resting order on the book.

createdBy.description: Present **only** when the order belongs to the caller.

sumUntaken.description: Win amount still available (taker pays risk to claim it).

takenRatio.description: Fraction of the order that has been matched.

gameStartExpiry.description: True when the order is set to auto-cancel at game start.

isPostArb.description: Present when the order was placed with `orderType: postArb`.

allOf.description: v2 orderbook: per-market arrays of orders (totals and spreads are flattened).
v1 orderbook: spreads and totals are objects keyed by spread / total number; values are arrays of orders.

odds.description: Best/average prices and max bet sizes for moneyline, spread, and total markets.

MatchedBet.description: A bet that has been matched but not yet graded.

UnmatchedOrder.description: An open order that is currently resting on the exchange.

otherParticipantID.description: The opposite participant id (the one whose side is being offered).

fee.description: Commission charged on the matched portion.

pinnacleLine.description: Snapshot of the comparable Pinnacle line at fill time, when available.

pnl.description: Sum of `result` across the returned wagers (rounded).

volume.description: Sum of `min(risk, win)` across the returned wagers (rounded).
